goal: Replace placeholder research-focus scores in the lab's evaluate hook with deterministic scores grounded in repo artifacts, run history, opportunity data, and portfolio maturity.
constraints: Do not fabricate additional run history to improve the score
behavioral_changes: Methodology scoring now derives from area-specific docs, packet matches, v3 dimension strength, and methodology run history.
Domain-discovery scoring now derives from ranked opportunity data, exploratory artifacts, and domain-discovery run history.
Transfer-pattern scoring now derives from transfer doctrine, packet volume, run history, and portfolio maturity.
AGI-theory scoring now derives from doctrine and empirical history but remains explicitly capped below production confidence.
Repeated evaluate calls in one process now reuse a cached repo-state snapshot.
follow_up: If evaluate-hook latency becomes a practical issue, isolate a cheaper repo-state summary from the full v3 scorer.
Extend future scoring changes by adding more repo-owned evidence rather than reintroducing constants.
